description: Contact Enrichment Integration: Build a pluggable enrichment service with built-in domain-based company lookup, role inference from title, email pattern generation + MX verification, bulk enrichment queue, and enrichment history per lead.
steps: Add Prisma models: `EnrichmentLog` (id, leadId, provider:String, requestData:Json, responseData:Json, status: 'pending'|'completed'|'failed', errorMessage?, createdAt, updatedAt). Add to schema.prisma with indexes on leadId and status.
Create Prisma migration for EnrichmentLog model.

Create `apps/api/src/services/enrichment/index.ts`: Enrichment service interface. Export `EnrichmentProvider` interface with `enrich(lead)` method. Export `enrichLead(leadId, providers?)` function that runs enabled providers, stores results in lead.enrichmentData, and logs to EnrichmentLog.

Create `apps/api/src/services/enrichment/domain-lookup.ts`: Provider that extracts domain from email, infers company info (name from domain, industry heuristics). Returns { company, domain, industry?, employeeRange? }.

Create `apps/api/src/services/enrichment/role-inference.ts`: Provider that analyzes lead title to infer seniority level (c-suite, vp, director, manager, individual), department (engineering, marketing, sales, hr, finance, operations), and decision-making authority score.

Create `apps/api/src/services/enrichment/email-finder.ts`: Service that given name + domain, generates common email patterns (first@domain, first.last@domain, firstlast@domain, f.last@domain, first_last@domain, etc.) and validates format. Export `findEmail(firstName, lastName, domain)` returning possible patterns with confidence scores. MX check is stubbed (no network) but interface is ready.

Create `apps/api/src/routes/enrichment.ts`: POST /api/leads/:id/enrich - enrich a single lead, POST /api/groups/:id/enrich - bulk enrich all leads in a group (queues a BullMQ job), GET /api/leads/:id/enrichment-history - get enrichment log for a lead, POST /api/leads/find-email - given name+domain find possible emails.
Create `apps/api/src/queue/enrichment-queue.ts`: BullMQ queue for bulk enrichment jobs. Worker processes leads in batches of 10 with delay between batches.
Add enrichment queue exports to apps/api/src/queue/index.ts.
Add shared types: ENRICHMENT_STATUS, SENIORITY_LEVEL, DEPARTMENT const arrays in packages/shared/src/types/index.ts.
Update mockDb.ts: Add `enrichmentLog` mock model. Add `buildEnrichmentLog()` factory in factories.ts.
Create `apps/api/src/services/enrichment/domain-lookup.test.ts`: Test domain extraction, company name inference.
Create `apps/api/src/services/enrichment/role-inference.test.ts`: Test title parsing for various titles (CEO, VP of Engineering, Sr. Software Engineer, etc.).
Create `apps/api/src/services/enrichment/email-finder.test.ts`: Test email pattern generation for various name+domain combinations.
Create `apps/api/src/routes/enrichment.test.ts`: Test all enrichment endpoints (single enrich, bulk enrich, history, find-email).
Register enrichment routes in apps/api/src/index.ts with requireAuth middleware.

Frontend: Create `apps/web/src/components/ui/EnrichmentPanel.tsx`: Panel shown on lead detail page displaying enrichment data (company info, role inference, confidence scores) with an 'Enrich' button.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/leads/:id/enrich enriches lead and stores results in enrichmentData field and EnrichmentLog
Domain lookup correctly extracts company name from email domain
Role inference correctly categorizes titles into seniority and department
Email finder generates valid email patterns for given name+domain
POST /api/groups/:id/enrich queues a bulk enrichment job
findings: All 48 enrichment tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly for shared, api, and web packages. The workers package has pre-existing build errors unrelated to this feature (rootDir issues in test imports). The api package has pre-existing TS errors in smtp-server.test.ts and simulation.test.ts also unrelated. Junior/associate seniority pattern placed before manager pattern to handle titles like 'Associate Product Manager' correctly.
